mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect deteriorating labor demand and contracting household income, which forward-discounts weaker consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS reported sharp sequential declines through late 2008/early 2009
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive-return frequency during PAYEMS decline months exceeds 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0%, the Federal Reserve has exhausted conventional rate-cutting capacity, signaling policymakers will pursue extraordinary easing (balance-sheet expansion), which historically precedes equity market stabilization and forward gains as liquidity floods risk assets over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS cut to near-zero range by December 2008
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns conditional on FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over the sample, or mean conditional return is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad-based collapse in both durable and non-durable consumer demand that compounds negative wealth and confidence effects, producing sharper negative equity returns than either series alone would predict over one month.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell to 1631k units and RSAFS declined sharply into early 2009
failure_condition: Joint HOUST/RSAFS decline signal fails to outperform single-series HOUST-only or RSAFS-only models on out-of-sample Rank IC by any positive margin over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a confirmed recessionary regime, a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) trend acts as a lagging but reinforcing signal of demand destruction, and continued UNRATE increases during recessions are associated with sustained negative equity drift over a quarterly horizon as earnings estimates are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE trending upward through 2008 amid NBER-dated recession
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward SPX return during periods of rising UNRATE within recession regimes is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or sign flips positive across multiple recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding a threshold indicates active supply-side contraction (factory shutdowns, inventory liquidation) that quickly feeds into short-horizon earnings warnings, producing negative equity returns within one trading week of the release.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO declining sharply on a YoY basis through late 2008/early 2009
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following INDPRO YoY breaches below -5% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirm a formal recession, triggering a systematic downward repricing of long-horizon equity risk premia as investors price in prolonged earnings compression over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: two consecutive quarters of negative GDPC1 growth
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 contracted in Q3 and Q4 2008
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns after confirmed two-quarter GDP contraction are positive in more than 50% of historical episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration of CPIAUCSL toward outright deflation raises real interest rates even as nominal rates fall, increasing the effective discount rate on equity cash flows and signaling demand destruction severe enough to depress corporate pricing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL inflation decelerated sharply into early 2009 amid falling energy prices
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns during CPI YoY < 1% episodes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the relationship reverses sign across at least two distinct low-inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near the zero lower bound while PAYEMS continues to decline, conventional monetary policy loses traction (liquidity trap), so equity markets cannot rely on rate cuts to offset labor deterioration, producing continued negative forward returns until unconventional easing measures visibly expand the balance sheet.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS near zero and PAYEMS falling concurrently in early 2009
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns during joint FEDFUNDS<1% and PAYEMS-declining regimes are not significantly more negative than periods with only one condition true (p > 0.10), or the joint signal underperforms either single-series proxy on Rank IC.
